name: Easter Lamb
recipeInstructions:
1. Obtain a leg of lamb and ask the butcher to bone it and butterfly it for you.
2. Do not let the butcher tie it in a roast; instead, request some butcher string so you can do it yourself.
3. When you receive the lamb at home, try to remove as much fat from the inside as possible if the butcher did not clean it well.
4. Lay the meat out flat on a large clean surface, skin side down.
5. Cut off the fuzzy top of the fennel and wash thoroughly. Cut into 4-inch stalks using everything (save the bulb).
6. Cover the meat first with salt and pepper. Spread the garlic all over the meat.
7. Lay the pieces of fennel next, spreading them over the meat.
8. Roll up like a jellyroll and tie about 2 inches apart using butcher string. Tie it lengthwise, tucking the ends in.
9. Place in a roasting pan and lightly oil the top and sides. Salt and pepper the roast.
10. Cut the fennel bulb into chunks and drizzle with about 2 tablespoons melted butter or olive oil.
11. Bake at 350°F for about 1 hour, covered, turning every half hour.
12. Remove the cover and continue to bake for another hour uncovered.
13. Before the last 30 minutes of baking, add 1/2 cup white wine.
14. When done, remove the meat to a warm platter. Do not cut too early; lamb gets cold quickly.
15. Place the roasting pan on top of the stove and make a gravy with the drippings. Add flour and water. You may even use a bit more wine or broth, adjusting for salt. Strain and serve on the side for the meat or mashed potatoes.
